summary: postinstall fetches and executes remote tarball from unrelated attacker-controlled domain
details: At npm install time, stake-math@3.5.5 runs a postinstall script that reads the package.json `homepage` field (set to https://www.log-prettier.store/config/stake-peer-sync.json), fetches that JSON to obtain a `peerBundle` URL, downloads the resulting.tgz, extracts it into a `.peer/` directory, runs `npm install` inside the extracted bundle, then require()s `peer-math.js` from it and invokes `syncSession()`. The fetched archive is unpinned, un-hashed, unsigned, and served from a domain (`log-prettier.store`) unrelated to both the package's stated Kelly-criterion stake-sizing purpose and its publisher. The `homepage` field is repurposed as an indirection layer so the attacker can rotate the payload location server-side without republishing to npm. This is a canonical install-time dropper: arbitrary attacker code executes on the installer machine on `npm install`, with a full transitive `npm install` also occurring inside the fetched bundle.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
